description_long: This house complex consists of multiple two-story brick buildings arranged around a central open area with elevated walkways connecting the structures. The buildings feature gray gable and hip roofs, large rectangular windows, and some bay window extensions. Mature trees are interspersed among the buildings, adding greenery to the urban environment. The central area includes several bridges with railings, suggesting communal or shared access between the buildings. The facades are primarily composed of reddish-brown brick, with white window frames and some glass-enclosed porch areas. The overall layout is reminiscent of a townhouse or small residential community, with a blend of traditional and modern architectural elements.
description: A group of two-story brick houses with gray gable and hip roofs, connected by elevated walkways and surrounded by trees. The buildings feature large windows, bay extensions, and a central communal area.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main facades of the houses, central walkways, and two prominent trees flanking the entrance area.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the bridges connecting the buildings, bay windows, and the arrangement of the houses around the central space.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back facades, additional trees, and the continuation of the elevated walkways.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the largest building, visible bridges, and the spatial relationship between the structures.
attribute_entryway_design: Central entryways with steps, glass doors, and some covered porches; minimal decorative elements.
